allowed_claims: BHSM includes local artifact-backed prediction adapters.
The interface distinguishes artifact-backed BHSM outputs from interface default/demo outputs.
Reference values are comparison inputs only, not BHSM derivation inputs.
artifact_backed_policy: Local machine-readable value plus explicit provenance; artifact presence alone does not close a theorem.
calibration_policy: A calibration anchor is not an independent prediction in the same run.
forbidden_claims: Artifact adapters independently predict W when W is used as anchor.
PDG/reference values are used as BHSM derivation inputs.
Missing artifacts are treated as predictions.
Interface placeholder formulas are treated as theorem-backed BHSM predictions.
X_ch is closed without an explicit artifact-backed theorem.
Neutrino physical basis/scale is closed without an explicit artifact-backed theorem.
Standalone CP O_int is closed without an explicit artifact-backed theorem.
interface_default_policy: Interface defaults remain labeled defaults and are not theorem-backed.
reference_value_policy: Reference values are comparison inputs only and never derivation inputs.
theorem_blocker_policy: X_ch, neutrino physical basis/scale, and standalone CP O_int remain blocked absent explicit theorem artifacts and callables.
